behavior: Gamma/Kling image+video model UI-names diverge from the API-accepted model strings: a UI-facing name (e.g. a 'kling-v2-6'-style label) is REJECTED by the API, which only accepts specific enum strings (kling-v1-6/v2-1-master/v2-master/v1). A styleguide image_model must carry the API string, never the UI display name.

developers.gamma.app burst-throttle empirically returns HTTP 401 'Invalid API key' rather than the documented 429 when the per-burst rate limit is exceeded. A 401 during a valid-key run is a throttle signal, not an auth failure; retry/backoff on 401 as if it were 429.
The video-generation account enforces a parallel-task cap: concurrent Kling generation tasks beyond the cap fail; text-to-video must be dispatched SEQUENTIALLY rather than fanned out in parallel.

DOCUMENTED: the scoped doc section enumerates the accepted values (https://developers.gamma.app/reference/image-model-accepted-values.md, fetched 2026-07-02T04:31:39Z). OBSERVED: enum app.specialists.gary._act:IMAGE_MODEL_VALUES vs documented tokens: enum-values-absent-from-docs=['imagen-4-fast', 'qwen-image', 'qwen-image-fast']; documented-values-absent-from-enum=['flux-1-pro', 'flux-1-quick', 'flux-1-ultra', 'flux-kontext-max', 'gpt-image-1-mini-high', 'gpt-image-1-mini-low', 'gpt-image-1-mini-medium', 'gpt-image-2', 'gpt-image-2-hd', 'gpt-image-2-mini', 'imagen-3-pro'] (kind: doc-drift). CONSEQUENCE: A styleguide or Gary dispatch carrying an enum-only model string may be rejected by the live API (or silently mis-generate), and documented cheaper/ newer models stay unusable until the frozen enum is reconciled by a human.

DOCUMENTED: the docs state '429 Too Many Requests' (https://developers.gamma.app/guides/async-patterns-and-polling.md, fetched 2026-07-02T04:31:41Z). OBSERVED: literal '429 Too Many Requests' present in the scoped section. The standing candidate obs-gamma-burst-throttle-401-not-429-2026-06-25 (live-observed reality) STANDS — re-confirmed against the live docs, which still carry the documented claim it contradicts. CONSEQUENCE: Retry/backoff keyed to a documented 429 misses the EMPIRICALLY OBSERVED 401 burst-throttle ('Invalid API key') — a valid-key 401 mid-run is a throttle signal, not an auth failure. Either resolution direction (docs corrected to 401, or docs still claiming 429 while the live candidate stands) is a genuine ledger write citing the standing candidate (S-1 / John J-1).
